title: pogogyne serpylloides var. typica
intro: Pogogyne serpylloides var. typica is a rare and endemic plant species native to California, United States, also known as California hyssop. It belongs to the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion.
